---
title: "Program Models, Theories of Change, and Qualitative Methods"
description: "Study note on Program Models, Theories of Change, and Qualitative Methods in Qualitative Research & Evaluation Methods."
book_id: "qualitative-research-evaluation-methods"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Major section"
source_pages: "314-316"
citation_key: "patton_2015_qualitative_research_evaluation_methods"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod23-module-23-evaluating-program-models-and-theories-of-change-and-evaluation-models:c0024"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

<div class="chapter-meta"><span>2015</span><span>MCP evaluation-texts</span><span>Status: needs_review</span><span>Source pages 314-316</span><span>Major section</span></div>
This note summarises the MCP research-library section "Program Models, Theories of Change, and Qualitative Methods" from Michael Quinn Patton's *Qualitative Research & Evaluation Methods* [@patton_2015_qualitative_research_evaluation_methods]. It is a paraphrased study note based on source-linked retrieval records, not a substitute for the source text.

## Core Argument

The section shows how qualitative inquiry can test and refine program models by connecting processes, implementation, and outcomes.

## Study Summary

The section shows how qualitative inquiry can test and refine program models by connecting processes, implementation, and outcomes.

Qualitative evidence is useful where the formal theory of change is incomplete, contested, or too abstract to explain practice.

This makes qualitative methods a complement to logic models: they can reveal how the model is experienced and enacted.

## Key Concepts

- Program model
- Theory of change
- Process study
- Implementation evidence

## Source Location

- Section path: Part 1 > Chapter 4 > Module 23: Evaluating Program Models and Theories of Change
- Evidence records: `chunk:qualitative-research-evaluation-methods:mod23-module-23-evaluating-program-models-and-theories-of-change-and-evaluation-models:c0024`

## Practical Implications

- Use interviews and observations to test whether the stated program model matches implementation.
- Look for mechanisms and unanticipated outcomes that the initial theory missed.
- Treat qualitative findings as evidence for revising the theory of change.

## Connections

- Book overview: [Overview](index.qmd)
- Concepts: [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd)
- Map: [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd)
- Practice synthesis: [Practice Implications](practice-implications.qmd)

## Study Prompts

1. What is the main evaluation problem that Program Models, Theories of Change, and Qualitative Methods helps diagnose?
2. Which assumptions would need evidence before using this guidance in a live evaluation?
3. How would this change the way an evaluator scopes questions, methods, or reporting?
4. Where could the guidance be misused if treated as a fixed template?

## References
